entries.section_title: Short title

metadata.summary: Declares the short title of the Act as the Penal Code 1871.

entries.section_title: “Man” and “woman”

metadata.summary: Defines “man” as a male human being of any age and “woman” as a female human being of any age.

entries.section_title: Right of private defence against deadly assault when there is risk of harm to innocent person

metadata.summary: This section extends the right of private defence to the unavoidable risk of harming an innocent person during a deadly assault.

entries.section_title: Start and continuance of right of private defence of body

metadata.summary: States when the right of private defence of the body begins and how long it continues.

entries.section_title: When right of private defence of body extends to causing death

metadata.summary: This section specifies the six descriptions of assault for which the right of private defence of the body extends, subject to sections 98 and 106A, to voluntarily causing death to the assailant.

entries.section_title: When such right extends to causing any harm other than death

metadata.summary: This section states that where section 102 does not apply, private defence of the body extends only to causing harm other than death, subject to sections 98 and 106A.

entries.section_title: Commencement and continuance of right of private defence of property

metadata.summary: States when the right of private defence of property begins and when it continues against theft, robbery, criminal trespass, mischief, and house-breaking.

entries.section_title: When right of private defence of property extends to causing death

metadata.summary: This section states when the right of private defence of property may extend to voluntarily causing death to the wrongdoer.

metadata.summary: Section 106 limits the right of private defence of property in certain theft, mischief, and criminal trespass cases to harm other than death, subject to sections 98 and 106A.

entries.section_title: Acts against which there is no right of private defence

metadata.summary: Section 106A removes the right of private defence against certain acts by public servants or acts done under their direction when the defender lacks a reasonable belief of death or grievous hurt.

entries.section_title: Abetment of the doing of a thing

metadata.summary: Section 107 defines abetment as instigation, conspiracy followed by an act or illegal omission in pursuance, or intentional aid, and states that impossibility unknown to the abettor does not prevent abetment.

entries.section_title: Abettor

metadata.summary: Defines who is an abettor and clarifies the scope of abetment through explanations and illustrations.

entries.section_title: Abetment in Singapore of an offence outside Singapore

metadata.summary: Defines abetment within the Penal Code where the abetting occurs in Singapore but the abetted act is committed outside Singapore and would be an offence if committed in Singapore.

entries.section_title: Abetment outside Singapore of an offence in Singapore

metadata.summary: Treats a person as abetting an offence within the Penal Code where the abetted offence is committed in Singapore even though any or all of the abetment acts were done outside Singapore.

entries.section_title: Punishment of abetment if the act abetted is committed in consequence, and where no express provision is made for its punishment

metadata.summary: This section applies the punishment of the abetted offence where the abetted act is committed in consequence of the abetment and no separate punishment provision governs that abetment.

entries.section_title: Person

metadata.summary: The word 'person' includes any company, association, or body of persons, incorporated or not.

entries.section_title: Punishment of abetment if the person abetted does the act with a different intention from that of the abettor

metadata.summary: An abettor is punished according to the offence that would have been committed if the act had been done with the abettor's intention or knowledge alone.

entries.section_title: Liability of abettor when one act is abetted and a different act is done

metadata.summary: This section makes an abettor liable for a different act that is done instead of the act abetted.

entries.section_title: Abettor, when liable to cumulative punishment for act abetted and for act done

metadata.summary: Section 112 makes an abettor liable to punishment for each offence where the additional act attracting section 111 liability is committed in addition to the act abetted and is a distinct offence.

entries.section_title: Liability of abettor for an offence caused by the act abetted different from that intended by the abettor

metadata.summary: An abettor who intended one effect is liable for a different effect actually caused by the abetted act if the abettor knew that different effect was likely.

entries.section_title: Abettor present when offence committed

metadata.summary: Section 114 deems an abettor who is present when the abetted act or offence is committed to have committed that act or offence.

entries.section_title: Abetment of offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life

metadata.summary: Section 115 punishes abetment of an offence punishable with death or life imprisonment where the offence is not committed and no other law expressly provides the punishment for that abetment.

entries.section_title: Abetment of offence punishable with imprisonment

metadata.summary: This section punishes abetment of an imprisonable offence that is not carried out, while allowing courts to depart from fixed or mandatory minimum sentences within the maximum punishment for the abetted offence.

entries.section_title: Abetting the commission of an offence by the public or by more than 10 persons

metadata.summary: This section criminalises abetting an offence to be committed by the public generally or by any number or class of persons exceeding 10.

entries.section_title: Concealing a design to commit an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life

metadata.summary: Concealing or falsely representing a design to commit an offence punishable with death or life imprisonment is an offence, with the imprisonment range depending on whether the planned offence is actually committed.

entries.section_title: A public servant concealing a design to commit an offence which it is his duty to prevent

metadata.summary: This section criminalises a public servant who, to facilitate an offence he is duty-bound to prevent, conceals the design to commit it or makes a knowingly false representation about that design.

entries.section_title: “Public”

metadata.summary: Defines “public” to include any class of the public or any community.

entries.section_title: Concealing a design to commit an offence punishable with imprisonment

metadata.summary: Concealing or falsely representing a design to commit an imprisonable offence, with intent or likely knowledge of facilitation, is punishable by a fraction of the predicate offence's imprisonment term or the predicate fine or both.

entries.section_title: Definition of criminal conspiracy

metadata.summary: Section 120A defines criminal conspiracy and clarifies when a person may still be a party despite impossibility, lack of intention by another party, or cross-border conduct.

entries.section_title: Punishment of criminal conspiracy

metadata.summary: Section 120B punishes a party to a criminal conspiracy, where the Code makes no express punishment provision for that conspiracy, in the same manner as if the person had abetted the offence that is the subject of the conspiracy.

entries.section_title: Waging or attempting to wage war or abetting the waging of war against the Government

metadata.summary: Section 121 makes it an offence to wage war against the Government, attempt to wage such war, or abet the waging of such war, punishable with death or imprisonment for life.

entries.section_title: Offences against the President’s person

metadata.summary: Planning the death of, hurt to, or unlawful imprisonment or restraint of the President is an offence punishable by life imprisonment or up to 20 years, with an uncapped fine if no life sentence is imposed.

entries.section_title: Offences against authority

metadata.summary: Planning the unlawful deprivation or deposition of the President from Singapore’s sovereignty, or the overawing of the Government by criminal force, is an offence punishable with life imprisonment or up to 20 years’ imprisonment and, if life imprisonment is not imposed, an unlimited fine.

entries.section_title: Abetting offences under section 121A or 121B

metadata.summary: This section makes it an offence to abet the commission of an offence punishable under section 121A or 121B, with punishment tracking the punishment for that offence.

entries.section_title: Intentional omission to give information of offences against section 121, 121A, 121B or 121C by a person bound to inform

metadata.summary: This section criminalises intentionally withholding legally required information about offences under sections 121, 121A, 121B or 121C despite knowing or having reason to believe such an offence has been committed.

entries.section_title: Collecting arms, etc., with the intention of waging war against the Government

metadata.summary: Collecting men, arms or ammunition, or otherwise preparing to wage war, with the intention of waging or being prepared to wage war against the Government is an offence punishable by life imprisonment or up to 20 years' imprisonment, with fine liability if life imprisonment is not imposed.

entries.section_title: Concealing with intent to facilitate a design to wage war

metadata.summary: Concealing by act or illegal omission the existence of a design to wage war against the Government, with intent or likely-knowledge that the concealment will facilitate that war, is punishable by up to 15 years' imprisonment and an uncapped fine.

entries.section_title: Assaulting President, etc., with intent to compel or restrain the exercise of any lawful power

metadata.summary: Section 124 criminalises assaulting, wrongfully restraining, or overawing the President or a Member of Parliament with intent to compel or restrain the exercise of lawful powers.

entries.section_title: Waging war against any power in alliance or at peace with Singapore

metadata.summary: Waging war against, attempting to wage war against, or abetting war against the government of a power allied with or at peace with Singapore is punishable with life imprisonment or up to 15 years' imprisonment, with an uncapped fine available on the non-life branch.

entries.section_title: Committing depredation on the territories of any power in alliance or at peace with Singapore

metadata.summary: Criminalises committing depredation, or making preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of a power in alliance or at peace with Singapore, with forfeiture of related property.

entries.section_title: Receiving property taken by war or depredation mentioned in sections 125 and 126

metadata.summary: Criminalises receiving property known to have been taken in the commission of the offences mentioned in sections 125 and 126, with forfeiture of that property.

entries.section_title: Public servant voluntarily allowing prisoner of State or war in his custody to escape

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence for a public servant with custody of a prisoner of State or war to voluntarily allow the prisoner to escape.

entries.section_title: Public servant negligently suffering prisoner of State or war in his custody to escape

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence for a public servant who has custody of a prisoner of State or prisoner of war to negligently allow that prisoner to escape.

entries.section_title: Aiding escape of, rescuing, or harbouring such prisoner

entries.section_title: Genocide

metadata.summary: Defines genocide as committing any listed genocidal act with intent to destroy, in whole or in part, a national, ethnical, racial, or religious group.

entries.section_title: Punishment for genocide

entries.section_title: Abetting mutiny, or attempting to seduce an officer or a serviceman from his duty

metadata.summary: Creates an offence for abetting mutiny by specified military personnel or attempting to seduce them from their allegiance or duty, punishable by life imprisonment or up to 10 years' imprisonment and a conditional uncapped fine.

entries.section_title: Abetment of mutiny, if mutiny is committed in consequence thereof

entries.section_title: Abetment of an assault by an officer or a serviceman on his superior officer, when in the execution of his office

metadata.summary: Criminalises abetting an assault by an officer or serviceman on a superior officer acting in the execution of his office.

entries.section_title: Abetment of such assault, if the assault is committed

metadata.summary: This section criminalises abetting the section 133 assault when that assault is actually committed in consequence of the abetment.

entries.section_title: Abetment of the desertion of an officer or a serviceman

metadata.summary: Criminalises abetting the desertion of an officer or a serviceman in the Singapore Armed Forces or visiting forces lawfully present in Singapore.

entries.section_title: Harbouring a deserter

metadata.summary: Criminalises harbouring a known deserter of the Singapore Armed Forces or visiting forces with intent to prevent apprehension.

entries.section_title: Deserter concealed on board merchant vessel through negligence of master

entries.section_title: Abetment of act of insubordination by an officer or a serviceman

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence to knowingly abet an act of insubordination by an officer or serviceman where that act is committed in consequence of the abetment.

entries.section_title: Saving

entries.section_title: Wearing the dress of a serviceman

metadata.summary: Criminalises a person who is not a serviceman wearing or carrying items resembling a serviceman's dress or token with intent to be believed to be such a serviceman.

entries.section_title: “Harbour”

metadata.summary: Defines “harbour” for Chapter 7 to include supplying aid or helping a person evade apprehension.

entries.section_title: Application of Chapter 7 to Singapore Police Force

metadata.summary: Applies Chapter 7 provisions concerning offences against armed forces personnel, with necessary modifications, to corresponding acts involving the Singapore Police Force and attached volunteer, auxiliary, or special forces.

entries.section_title: Unlawful assembly

metadata.summary: Defines when an assembly of 5 or more persons becomes an unlawful assembly by reason of its common object.

entries.section_title: Being a member of an unlawful assembly

metadata.summary: Defines when a person is said to be a member of an unlawful assembly.

entries.section_title: Punishment

metadata.summary: Prescribes imprisonment up to 2 years, a fine, or both for being a member of an unlawful assembly.

entries.section_title: Joining an unlawful assembly armed with any deadly weapon

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence to be a member of an unlawful assembly while armed with a deadly weapon or something likely to cause death if used as a weapon of offence.

entries.section_title: Joining or continuing in an unlawful assembly, knowing that it has been commanded to disperse

metadata.summary: Criminalises joining or continuing in an unlawful assembly while knowing it has been lawfully commanded to disperse.

entries.section_title: Force used by one member in prosecution of common object

metadata.summary: Defines rioting where force or violence is used by an unlawful assembly or any of its members in prosecution of the assembly's common object.

entries.section_title: Punishment for rioting

metadata.summary: Prescribes punishment for a person guilty of rioting under section 146.

entries.section_title: Rioting, armed with a deadly weapon

entries.section_title: Every member of an unlawful assembly to be deemed guilty of any offence committed in prosecution of common object

metadata.summary: Deems every member of an unlawful assembly guilty of an offence committed in prosecution of the assembly's common object or known likely in its prosecution.

entries.section_title: Hiring, or conniving at hiring, of persons to join an unlawful assembly

metadata.summary: Makes a person liable where the person hires, engages, employs, promotes, or connives at recruiting another person to join an unlawful assembly.

entries.section_title: Knowingly joining or continuing in any assembly of 5 or more persons after it has been commanded to disperse

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence to knowingly join or remain in a 5-person assembly likely to disturb the peace after a lawful dispersal command.

entries.section_title: Assaulting or obstructing public servant when suppressing riot, etc.

metadata.summary: Assaulting, threatening, obstructing, or using criminal force against a public servant performing duty to disperse an unlawful assembly or suppress a riot or affray is punishable.

entries.section_title: Intentionally or rashly giving provocation, with intent to cause riot

metadata.summary: Section 153 criminalises intentionally or rashly giving illegal provocation with intent or knowledge that it is likely to cause rioting, with punishment depending on whether rioting follows.

entries.section_title: Owner or occupier of land on which an unlawful assembly is held

entries.section_title: Harbouring persons hired for an unlawful assembly

entries.section_title: Personation of an assessor

metadata.summary: A person commits an offence by unlawfully getting himself returned, empanelled or sworn as an assessor, or by voluntarily serving as an assessor after knowing that status was obtained contrary to law.

entries.section_title: “Fault element” and “physical element”

entries.section_title: “Fraudulently”

metadata.summary: Defines when a person is said to do an act fraudulently, including the slight-advantage exception and pleading rule.

entries.section_title: Counterfeiting a Government stamp

metadata.summary: Creates the offence of counterfeiting a Government revenue stamp or knowingly performing part of that counterfeiting process, and extends stamp references to stamp certificates.

entries.section_title: Having possession of an instrument or material for the purpose of counterfeiting a Government stamp

metadata.summary: This section criminalises possessing any instrument or material for use, or intended use, in counterfeiting a Government revenue stamp.

entries.section_title: Making or selling an instrument for the purpose of counterfeiting a Government stamp

metadata.summary: This section criminalises making, partly making, buying, selling, or disposing of an instrument intended for use in counterfeiting a Government revenue stamp.

entries.section_title: Sale of counterfeit Government stamp

metadata.summary: Selling or offering to sell a stamp known or believed to counterfeit a Government revenue stamp is an offence.

entries.section_title: Using a Government stamp known to have been before used

metadata.summary: Using a Government revenue stamp that is known to have been previously used, fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to the Government, is an offence.

entries.section_title: Erasure of mark denoting that stamp has been used

metadata.summary: This section criminalises erasing a used mark from a Government revenue stamp, or possessing or disposing of such erased or used stamps, when done fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to the Government.

entries.section_title: Affray

entries.section_title: “Intentionally”

metadata.summary: Defines knowingly for circumstances and effects, and states when knowledge is established by intention or wilful blindness.

entries.section_title: “Rashly”

metadata.summary: Defines when an act is done rashly in respect of a circumstance or effect, and when intention or knowledge also establishes rashness as a fault element.

entries.section_title: “Negligently”

metadata.summary: Defines 'negligently' as omitting/doing an act contrary to how a reasonable person would act.

entries.section_title: “Transferred fault”

metadata.summary: Sets out when intention or knowledge can transfer where an accused's act or effect concerns a different person or thing, while preserving proof of all fault elements and available defences.

entries.section_title: “Strict liability”

metadata.summary: Defines strict liability for offences and physical elements, and states the reasonable-care defence for strict liability offences.

entries.section_title: Property in possession of spouse, clerk or servant

metadata.summary: Property in possession of a person's spouse, clerk or servant on the person's account is in that person's possession.

entries.section_title: Intentional or rash act likely to spread infection of any disease dangerous to life

metadata.summary: Criminalises intentionally or rashly doing an act known or believed to be likely to spread infection of a disease dangerous to life.

entries.section_title: Disobedience to a quarantine rule

metadata.summary: Criminalises knowingly disobeying a lawfully made and promulgated quarantine rule governing vessels or intercourse with infected places.

entries.section_title: Adulteration of food or drink which is intended for sale

metadata.summary: Criminalises adulterating food or drink so that it becomes noxious where it is intended or known to be likely to be sold as food or drink.

entries.section_title: Sale of noxious food or drink

metadata.summary: Criminalises selling, offering, or exposing for sale any food or drink article that is noxious or unfit, knowing or having reason to believe it is noxious as food or drink.

entries.section_title: Adulteration of drugs

metadata.summary: Criminalises adulterating any drug or medical preparation so that it is less efficacious, operates differently, or becomes noxious, where it is intended or likely to be sold or used for medicinal purposes as unadulterated.

entries.section_title: Sale of adulterated drugs

metadata.summary: Criminalises knowingly selling, offering, dispensing as unadulterated, or causing medicinal use of an adulterated drug or medical preparation.

entries.section_title: Sale of any drug as a different drug or preparation

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence knowingly to sell, offer, expose, or issue any drug or medical preparation as a different drug or medical preparation.

entries.section_title: Fouling the water of a public spring or reservoir

metadata.summary: Criminalises voluntarily corrupting or fouling the water of a public spring or reservoir so that it becomes less fit for its ordinary use.

entries.section_title: Making atmosphere noxious to health

metadata.summary: Creates an offence of voluntarily vitiating the atmosphere in any place so that it becomes noxious to the health of persons in the neighbourhood or passing along a public way.

entries.section_title: Rash driving or riding on a public way

metadata.summary: Creates an offence for driving a vehicle or riding on a public way in a rash or negligent manner that endangers human life or is likely to cause hurt or injury to another person.

entries.section_title: “Counterfeit”

metadata.summary: Defines counterfeiting as causing one thing to resemble another with deceptive intent or knowledge, with rules on exact imitation and a rebuttable presumption.

entries.section_title: Rash navigation of a vessel

metadata.summary: Criminalises navigating a vessel rashly or negligently in a way that endangers human life or is likely to cause hurt or injury to another person.

entries.section_title: Exhibition of a false light, mark or buoy

metadata.summary: Criminalises exhibiting a false light, mark or buoy with intent or knowledge that it is likely to mislead any navigator.

entries.section_title: Conveying person by water for hire in a vessel overloaded or unsafe

metadata.summary: Criminalises knowingly or negligently conveying a person by water for hire in a vessel that is unsafe or overloaded so as to endanger that person’s life.

entries.section_title: Danger or obstruction in a public way or navigation

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence to cause danger, obstruction or injury in a public way or public line of navigation by an act or by failing to control property in one's possession or charge.

entries.section_title: Rash or negligent conduct with respect to dangerous or harmful substance

metadata.summary: Creates an offence for rash or negligent acts or omissions involving dangerous or harmful substances, with penalties scaled to the harm or risk caused.

entries.section_title: Causing or contributing to risk of dangerous fire

metadata.summary: Creates an offence for rashly or negligently causing or substantially contributing to the risk of a fire that occurs and creates one of six specified harmful outcomes, with punishment scaled to the outcome.

entries.section_title: Presumption of cause of fire

metadata.summary: Creates a rebuttable presumption in section 285 proceedings where a listed fire-causing item is deposited and a nearby fire occurs within 60 minutes.

entries.section_title: Rash or negligent conduct with respect to any machinery in possession or under charge of offender

entries.section_title: Grievous hurt

metadata.summary: Defines the exhaustive categories of hurt that are legally designated as grievous hurt.

entries.section_title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt

metadata.summary: Whoever does any act with the intention of thereby causing hurt to any person, or with the knowledge that he is likely thereby to cause hurt to any person, and does thereby cause hurt to any person, is said voluntarily to cause hurt.

entries.section_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt

metadata.summary: Defines when a person voluntarily causes grievous hurt by voluntarily causing hurt with the requisite intention or knowledge and actually causing grievous hurt.

entries.section_title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt

metadata.summary: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine which may extend to $5,000, or with both.

entries.section_title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt which causes grievous hurt

metadata.summary: Defines criminal force as intentional non-consensual force used with the required offence purpose, illegal intent, or knowledge.

entries.section_title: Assault

metadata.summary: Defines assault as a gesture or preparation that intentionally or knowingly causes apprehension of imminent criminal force.

entries.section_title: Punishment for using criminal force otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation

metadata.summary: Provides the punishment for assault or criminal force committed otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation, and states when provocation does not mitigate punishment.

entries.section_title: Using criminal force to deter a public servant from discharge of his duty

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence to assault or use criminal force against a public servant in the execution of duty, to deter that duty, or in retaliation for lawful discharge of that duty.

entries.section_title: Assault or Criminal Force to Outrage Modesty

metadata.summary: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage the modesty of that person, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 5 years, or with fine, or with caning, or with any combination of such punishments.

entries.section_title: Outraging modesty in certain circumstances

metadata.summary: This section criminalises using or attempting specified violence, restraint, or threats in order to commit or facilitate an offence under section 354, with enhanced punishment if done in a lift or against a person below 14 years of age.

entries.section_title: Assault or criminal force with intent to dishonour otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation

metadata.summary: This section criminalises assault or use of criminal force intended to dishonour a person, unless it was done on grave and sudden provocation given by that person.

entries.section_title: Assault or criminal force in committing or attempting to commit theft of property carried by a person

metadata.summary: Section 356 criminalises assault or criminal force used on a person while committing or attempting to commit theft of property that the person is wearing or carrying.

entries.section_title: Assault or criminal force in attempting wrongfully to confine a person

metadata.summary: This section criminalises assault or criminal force used while attempting wrongfully to confine the same person.

entries.section_title: Assaulting or using criminal force on grave and sudden provocation

metadata.summary: This section punishes assault or use of criminal force against a person when that person gave grave and sudden provocation.

entries.section_title: Kidnapping

metadata.summary: Section 359 classifies kidnapping into kidnapping from Singapore and kidnapping from lawful guardianship.

entries.section_title: Effect caused partly by act and partly by omission

metadata.summary: Where causing an effect is an offence, causing it partly by act and partly by omission is the same offence.

entries.section_title: Kidnapping from Singapore

metadata.summary: Section 360 defines kidnapping from Singapore as conveying a person beyond Singapore without the required consent.

entries.section_title: Kidnapping from lawful guardianship

metadata.summary: Section 361 defines kidnapping from lawful guardianship where a minor below 16 or a person of unsound mind is taken or enticed out of a lawful guardian’s keeping without that guardian’s consent.

entries.section_title: Abduction

metadata.summary: Defines abduction as compelling by force or inducing by deceit any person to go from any place.

entries.section_title: Punishment for Kidnapping

metadata.summary: Whoever kidnaps any person from Singapore or from lawful guardianship, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to 10 years, and shall also be liable to fine or to caning.

entries.section_title: Punishment for abduction

metadata.summary: Section 489B makes it an offence to deal in or use forged or counterfeit currency or bank notes as genuine while knowing or having reason to believe they are forged or counterfeit.

entries.section_title: Possession of forged or counterfeit currency or bank notes

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence to possess forged or counterfeit currency or bank notes while knowing or having reason to believe they are forged or counterfeit and intending that they be used as genuine.

entries.section_title: Making or possessing instruments or materials for forging or counterfeiting currency or bank notes

metadata.summary: This section makes it an offence to make, deal in, or possess instruments or materials intended for use in forging or counterfeiting currency or bank notes.

entries.section_title: Abetting in Singapore counterfeiting of currency out of Singapore

metadata.summary: This section makes it an offence for a person within Singapore to abet the counterfeiting of any currency outside Singapore, punishable in the same manner as equivalent abetment within Singapore.

entries.section_title: Fraudulently or dishonestly diminishing weight or altering composition of any coin

metadata.summary: Fraudulently or dishonestly diminishing the weight of a coin or altering its composition is punishable by up to 7 years' imprisonment and an uncapped fine.

entries.section_title: Altering appearance of currency with intent that it shall pass as currency of different description

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence to alter the appearance of currency intending it to pass as currency of a different description.

entries.section_title: Delivery to another of altered currency

metadata.summary: This section criminalises delivering altered currency as genuine or as a different currency, or attempting to induce another person to receive it, while knowing or having reason to believe it was altered under section 489F or 489G.

entries.section_title: Possession of altered currency

metadata.summary: Section 489I criminalises possessing currency known or believed to have been altered through a section 489F or 489G offence with intent that it be used as genuine.

entries.section_title: “Year” and “month”

metadata.summary: The words “year” and “month” are to be reckoned according to the Gregorian calendar.

entries.section_title: Defamation

metadata.notes: Speech-based offense demonstrating exception/defense handling for truth, public good, and good faith
metadata.summary: Whoever, by words either spoken or intended to be read, or by signs, or by visible representations, makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person, intending to harm, or knowing or having reason to believe that such imputation will harm the reputation of such person, is said, except in the cases hereinafter excepted, to defame that person.

entries.section_title: Offences against State and genocide committed outside Singapore by citizen or permanent resident

metadata.summary: Singapore citizens and permanent residents who commit overseas acts or omissions that would be Chapter 6 or 6B offences in Singapore are deemed to have committed them in Singapore.

entries.section_title: Punishment of specified offences with elements occurring in Singapore but others occurring outside Singapore

metadata.summary: Deems specified offences to have been committed in Singapore when the statutory territorial or harm-location conditions are met.

entries.section_title: Certain laws not to be affected by this Code

metadata.summary: Preserves existing military discipline laws and other in-force laws from being repealed, varied, suspended, or affected by the Penal Code.

entries.section_title: Section

metadata.summary: Defines 'section' as a numbered portion of a Chapter of the Code.

entries.section_title: Punishment for defamation

metadata.summary: Prescribes up to 2 years’ imprisonment, an uncapped fine, or both for a person who defames another.

entries.section_title: Printing or engraving matter known to be defamatory

metadata.summary: Section 501 makes it an offence to print or engrave matter known or believed to be defamatory of a person.

entries.section_title: Sale of printed or engraved substance containing defamatory matter

metadata.summary: Selling or offering for sale a printed or engraved substance containing defamatory matter, while knowing it contains that matter, is an offence punishable by up to 2 years' imprisonment, a fine, or both.

entries.section_title: Criminal Breach of Trust

metadata.notes: Fiduciary offense demonstrating trust relationship and breach elements
metadata.summary: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use that property, or dishonestly uses or disposes of that property in violation of any direction of law prescribing the mode in which such trust is to be discharged, or of any legal contract, express or implied, which he has made touching the discharge of such trust, or wilfully suffers any other person so to do, commits criminal breach of trust.

entries.section_title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke a breach of the peace

metadata.summary: This section criminalises intentionally insulting a person in a way that provokes that person, where the accused intends or knows the provocation is likely to cause a breach of the public peace or another offence.

entries.section_title: Statements conducing to public mischief

coverage.L3_verified_by: Opus 4.7 (automated reviewer, phase D priority batch)

metadata.summary: Criminalises making, publishing or circulating statements, rumours or reports that intend or are likely to cause mutiny, public fear leading to offences, or inter-class or inter-community offences.

entries.section_title: Punishment for criminal intimidation

metadata.summary: Provides the punishment for criminal intimidation, with a higher sentencing tier for specified aggravated threats.

entries.section_title: Criminal intimidation by an anonymous communication

metadata.summary: Makes it an offence to commit criminal intimidation through an anonymous communication or by concealing the source of the threat, with up to 2 years’ additional imprisonment on top of section 506.

entries.section_title: “Oath”

metadata.summary: Defines “oath” to include legally substituted solemn affirmations and declarations required or authorised by law for public-servant or proof purposes.

entries.section_title: Attempt to commit offence

coverage.L3_verified_by: Opus 4.7 (automated reviewer, phase D priority batch)

metadata.summary: Section 511 defines an attempt as intending to commit an offence and taking a substantial step towards it, including where unknown facts make completion impossible.

entries.section_title: Punishment for attempting to commit offences

metadata.summary: Section 512 sets the punishment rules for attempts where no other express attempt-punishment provision applies, including a special rule for minimum sentences.

entries.section_title: Punishments

metadata.summary: Enumerates the punishments to which offenders are liable under the Penal Code.

entries.section_title: Imprisonment for life

metadata.summary: Defines 'imprisonment for life' as imprisonment for the duration of a person's natural life.

entries.section_title: Definitions in this Code to be understood subject to exceptions

metadata.summary: All offence definitions and penal provisions are read subject to the General Exceptions and Right of Private Defence chapters.

entries.section_title: Definitions to apply to this Code and other written law

metadata.summary: Definitions of words or expressions explained in sections 22A to 26H apply to offences in the Code and other written law unless expressly displaced, except for dishonestly and fraudulently.

entries.section_title: Expression once explained is used in the same sense throughout this Code

metadata.summary: Expressions explained anywhere in the Code carry that explanation throughout the Code.

entries.section_title: Punishment of a person found guilty of one of several offences, the judgment stating that it is doubtful of which

metadata.summary: Where judgment finds guilt for one of several specified offences but leaves doubt about which offence, punishment is for the offence carrying the lowest punishment unless all carry the same punishment.

entries.section_title: Enhanced penalties for offences against domestic workers

metadata.summary: Allows enhanced sentencing for specified Penal Code offences committed against domestic workers by employers, household members, or employment agents.

entries.section_title: Enhanced penalties for racially or religiously aggravated offences

metadata.summary: Allows a court to double the otherwise applicable punishment for specified Penal Code offences that are racially or religiously aggravated.

entries.section_title: Enhanced penalties for offences against vulnerable persons

metadata.summary: Allows enhanced punishment for specified Penal Code offences committed against vulnerable persons when the offender knew or ought reasonably to have known of the vulnerability.

entries.section_title: Enhanced penalties for offences against person below 14 years of age

metadata.summary: Allows the court to impose up to twice the otherwise available maximum punishment for qualifying Penal Code offences committed against persons below 14 years of age.

entries.section_title: Enhanced penalties for offences against victims in intimate relationships

metadata.summary: Allows the court to impose up to twice the otherwise available maximum punishment for certain Chapter 16 offences against victims in intimate relationships.

entries.section_title: Enhanced penalties for offences against victims in close relationships

metadata.summary: Allows the court to impose up to twice the maximum punishment for eligible Chapter 16 offences against victims in close relationships.

entries.section_title: Application of enhanced penalties
